title: Optimize status WIP

tasks.title: Add typed status recommendation model and grouping builder
tasks.verification: RED: bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/status-recommendations.test.ts failed on missing module. GREEN: same command passed (2 tests).
tasks.implementation_summary: Added `status-recommendations.ts` with structural recommendation kinds, priorities, sources, item/group/summary types, deterministic grouping order, per-group omitted counts, total omitted count, drilldown hints, and legacy string projection helper. Added RED/GREEN unit tests for group ordering and omission accounting.
tasks.notes: Typed recommendation grouping model implemented and checkpointed at 69b8530dbf0070020dc110711531d3596aec3580.

tasks.title: Migrate status recommendation producers to structural helper
tasks.verification: RED: targeted status test failed because appendRecencyRecommendation accepted only string[]. GREEN: targeted status test passed after structural recommendation carrier/helper migration.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added `StatusRecommendationCarrier` plus `pushStatusRecommendation` helper in status tooling. Migrated recency, clarify, next-gate enrichment, queue serviceability, search attribute, session-debt, health-snapshot, and archived-branch hygiene recommendations to emit typed structural items while preserving legacy flat recommendation strings. Added regression proving recency producer emits `stale` structural kind.
tasks.notes: Producer structural migration checkpointed at ec0fb9d034cbd5a8e3aee666e54e2d93dce1f410.

tasks.title: Add grouped/bounded status view projections
tasks.verification: RED: summary high-WIP test failed because recommendation_summary was undefined. GREEN: targeted summary test passed after grouped fields were projected.
tasks.implementation_summary: Added view-level recommendation grouping projections. `adv_status` now builds `recommendation_summary`/`recommendation_groups` from the full typed item set before flat recommendation truncation, exposes those additive fields across status views, and caps detailed flat recommendation lists above a named threshold. Extended high-WIP summary fixture to assert grouped fields and omitted counts while preserving existing recent-change and flat recommendation compatibility.
tasks.notes: Grouped/bounded status view projections checkpointed at ffe37eaa3c9f906834ac3611a007f00abe20df53.

tasks.title: Render compact grouped next-actions in formatted status output
tasks.verification: RED: formatter test failed because nextActionsSection was undefined. GREEN: targeted formatter test passed after grouped next-actions rendering and status call-site wiring.
tasks.implementation_summary: Extended formatted status output with a pure `nextActionsSection` that renders grouped recommendation counts, top examples, omitted counts, and drilldown hints. Wired `recommendationSummary` from `adv_status` into `formatStatusOutput` while keeping legacy `recommendationsList` intact. Added RED/GREEN formatter coverage for grouped next-actions.
tasks.notes: Formatted grouped next-actions checkpointed at d016224607bb14be22cbfbf229d136cb436f0897.

tasks.title: Verify status WIP behavior and compatibility
tasks.verification: Acceptance reviewer remediation: archived-branch hygiene now preserves typed recommendation_items; regression assertion added. Reviewer verification passed: status tests, status-recommendations/tool-formatters tests, typecheck, check, git diff --check.
tasks.implementation_summary: Verified grouped/bounded status behavior and compatibility. Targeted status/formatter tests passed (83 tests). `pnpm run check` passed after formatting. Acceptance reviewer then fixed one issue: archived-branch hygiene now preserves typed `recommendation_items`, with regression assertion. Reviewer verification passed status tests, status-recommendations/tool-formatters tests, typecheck, check, and git diff --check.
tasks.notes: Verification complete, plus acceptance reviewer remediation checkpointed at ee56f4c350b02f0e4ac9a479a1a77de0ef6b80a0.

error_recovery.last_error: `pnpm run check` failed on Prettier format:check for status-recommendations.ts/status.ts plus pre-existing cli-bridge-contract.test.ts/change.ts.

attempts.error: format:check failed for src/cli-bridge-contract.test.ts, src/tools/change.ts, src/tools/status-recommendations.ts, src/tools/status.ts

subagent_reports.topic: Design validation for optimizeStatusWip (high-WIP status output grouping/bounding)

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/status.ts (recommendation producers + view projection)
sources.summary: Confirms recommendations are appended as free-text strings from getRecommendationForGate (line 598), appendClarifyRecommendation (713), appendRecencyRecommendation (730/741), pushQueueServiceabilityRecommendations (1228+), appendArchivedBranchHygieneRecommendations (1260+), and inline pushes (config/external-state warnings 1476/1488). capSummaryRecommendations (807) runs only for summary view at line 1683, BEFORE applyStatusView (1886). applyStatusView reads full.recommendations for summary/health/changes/hygiene; only summary sets recommendations_omitted from statusSummaryOmissions.

sources.label: plugin/src/utils/tool-formatters.ts (FormattedStatus / StatusInput / formatStatusOutput)
sources.summary: FormattedStatus.recommendationsList is a pure passthrough of input.recommendations (line 532). formatStatusOutput is a pure formatter over already-loaded data; adding a grouped next-actions section here is additive and boundary-safe. No grouped section exists today.

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/status.test.ts (existing high-WIP + view tests)
sources.summary: Summary cap test pins recommendations length 11 (10 + omission line) and recommendations_omitted=5 from a 15-item array. changes view pins length 15 uncapped, recommendations_omitted undefined. appendRecencyRecommendation is exported via _test for direct unit testing. These are the compatibility anchors any additive change must preserve.

sources.label: AGENTS.md — enforced layer boundaries
sources.summary: utils/context-snapshot.ts purity guard (context-snapshot.purity.test.ts) forbids utils/ -> storage/ or utils/ -> tools/ imports. A pure grouping builder over already-loaded typed items is boundary-safe in either tools/status.ts or a new pure module; it must not import storage/tools.

sources.label: chat-output-display spec v1.6.0
sources.summary: Governs context snapshots, tickers, status markers, terminal title — NOT the recommendation list. No law constrains recommendation grouping/bounds, so additive grouped fields + a formatted next-actions section introduce no spec conflict. rq-ctxsnap2/rq-ctxticker2.5 still require adv_status to emit primary full-box snapshot + non-primary tickers; design does not touch that path.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: The design follows the canonical read-model / projection pattern: classify structurally at the generation site (where typed evidence exists), then group/rank once in a pure deterministic builder, and project bounded views. This is the boring, correct approach and is consistent with project wisdom (compact shaping in typed builders, not UI heuristics) cited in the agreement. Source inspection confirms feasibility: (1) every current producer call site is enumerable and each has structured evidence available at emission time (gate id, change id, findings count, minutesSinceActivity, queue/worker health, hygiene counts), so D1/D5 mapping is achievable without string parsing; (2) flat `recommendations: string[]` remains a trivial projection from typed items, satisfying AC6 backward-compat; (3) the formatter is already a pure passthrough, so a grouped next-actions section is purely additive (AC5); (4) the pure grouping builder respects the enforced layer boundary as long as it stays a pure function over already-loaded items. No spec conflict: chat-output-display does not legislate the recommendation list.

validation.notes: No blocking conflicts. Design is feasible and consistent with current source and specs. Verdict READY with required corrections that are small/structural, not architectural. Key compatibility traps to honor are in status.test.ts (summary length 11 + recommendations_omitted=5; changes length 15 uncapped) and the cap-before-view ordering at status.ts:1683 vs applyStatusView at 1886.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to design gate (READY) with these required corrections recorded in design/planning: (R1) Pin the summary double-bound interaction. capSummaryRecommendations already mutates status.recommendations to length 11 before applyStatusView; the new typed grouping must derive recommendation_groups/recommendation_summary from the FULL typed item set (pre-cap), not from the truncated flat strings, otherwise omitted counts will be wrong. Build typed items + groups first, then project the legacy flat cap from them. (R2) Make all numeric bounds named constants with tests (design already says this in D4 — keep it a hard AC, no prose-only limits). (R3) Decide the legacy string->kind fallback's authority explicitly: per P33/agreement constraint it must be display-only, never correctness; add a test asserting all known producers emit structural kinds and that the fallback path is unreachable for them. (R4) Preserve exported _test surface (appendRecencyRecommendation) or migrate its tests deliberately; existing tests at status.test.ts:503-546 call it directly. (R5) Confirm placement of the pure builder keeps the utils purity guard green if placed under utils/ (no storage/tools imports); placing it in tools/status.ts or a new pure tools-local/types-only module avoids the guard entirely.
follow_ups: Nonblocking: design's own Risk note acknowledges hygiene view can still truncate via non-recommendation sections (external_state_hygiene, opencode_session_debt, project_metadata). This change scopes recommendations only; surface a follow-up change if those structures still dominate truncation on PokeEdge-scale input after recommendation grouping lands.

Nonblocking: blocked_or_stuck / release_ready classification (D5 last rows) depends on per-change gate/readiness data that summary view intentionally avoids hydrating for all changes (cost). Recommend sourcing these only from changes already enriched in enrichRecentChangeStatus (status.ts:616) to avoid expensive all-change hydration; document this bound so AC4 deterministic-class-assignment is not interpreted as requiring full hydration in summary.

Nonblocking: formatStatusOutput summary view already blanks health/worktree/session/peer sections (status.ts:1813-1817). Ensure the new grouped next-actions section is included in summary's retained sections, else summary loses the grouped synthesis the change is meant to add.

Nonblocking: add a tool-formatters.test.ts fixture for the grouped next-actions section (formatter currently only tested for recommendationsList length 0/1); the design's formatter RED test should land here.
Packet note: TASK_SCOPE/IN_SCOPE/OUT_OF_SCOPE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION anchors were not provided in the orchestrator packet; continued with prompt scope per policy.

nonblocking_findings.why: Groups sort by explicit operational kind order, items sort by priority/activity/change/title, and summary omitted counts are derived from typed recommendation items rather than parsing legacy strings.

nonblocking_findings.label: praise
nonblocking_findings.what: Formatted status output preserves formatter purity while adding compact grouped next-actions.
nonblocking_findings.why: Formatter consumes already-structured `recommendationSummary` input, performs no store access, and keeps legacy `recommendationsList` intact for compatibility.

changes_made.summary: Fixed archived-branch hygiene recommendation propagation so typed `recommendation_items` survive through the summary/hygiene branch and appear in `recommendation_groups`, not only legacy flat `recommendations`.
changes_made.verification: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/status.test.ts` passed 47/47; `pnpm --dir plugin run typecheck` passed; `pnpm --dir plugin run check` passed.

changes_made.summary: Added regression assertion that summary view includes archived-branch cleanup in grouped recommendations when the legacy cleanup-ready recommendation is emitted.

wisdom_candidates.content: When a status recommendation helper operates on a temporary carrier object, pass through both `recommendations` and `recommendation_items`; otherwise legacy flat output can look correct while grouped typed summaries silently omit that producer.

verification.evidence: Path preflight passed for all scoped files. Targeted status suite passed: 47 tests. Targeted recommendation/formatter suites passed: 36 tests across 2 files. Typecheck passed. Full check pipeline passed: schemas:check, typecheck, test-isolation check, lockfile policy, lint, format:check. `git diff --check` reported no whitespace errors.

wisdom.content: For status recommendation migrations, preserve legacy flat strings by storing an optional `message` on the typed item and deriving `recommendations` from the typed projection. This lets tests and existing JSON consumers keep exact strings while grouped status uses structural `kind/source/priority` fields.

For bounded status projections, compute grouped summaries from the full typed recommendation set before applying any flat-list caps. Otherwise omitted counts and grouped actionability inherit summary truncation and become wrong under high-WIP load.

`pnpm run check` can surface pre-existing Prettier drift outside touched scope after a narrow targeted edit. If format:check fails only on unrelated files, formatting them may be the least risky path to satisfy required check, but call it out in verification/summary as campsite formatting rather than feature scope.

Incorporated validator refinements: build groups before flat truncation, named constants/tests, display-only legacy fallback, preserve appendRecencyRecommendation test surface or deliberate migration, pure builder boundary.

documents.proposal:
# Optimize status WIP

## Why

Large ADV projects now make `adv_status` hard to use. PokeEdge has 134 in-flight changes and 169 archived; PokeEdge-Web has 61 in-flight and 104 archived. `adv_status view:"hygiene"` and related summaries truncate before the user gets a useful synthesis, while recommendations become long repetitive stale-change lists.

## Problem Statement

`adv_status` needs a large-WIP mode that preserves actionable signal under high active-change counts without dumping hundreds of stale recommendations or forcing agents to manually page and synthesize state.

## What Changes

Improve the status/read path for large WIP sets so it returns bounded, ranked, grouped, and task-oriented output. The tool should surface immediate actions, hygiene risks, blocked/release-ready work, and counts with drill-down pointers rather than full repeated lists.

## User Outcomes

- Users can ask "where are we?" on high-WIP projects and get a concise, useful answer.
- Agents can identify top unblock/archive/cleanup work without tripping output truncation.
- Status remains fast and safe for both current-project and `target_path` snapshots.
- Detailed data remains available through explicit views/pagination.

## Scope

### In Scope

- `adv_status` summary/hygiene/changes output shaping for high-WIP projects.
- Recommendation grouping/deduplication/ranking for stale, release-ready, ambiguity, cleanup, and blocked classes.
- Bounded output contracts with totals, omitted counts, and explicit drill-down commands/tool hints.
- Tests/fixtures using PokeEdge-scale state shapes or synthetic equivalents.
- Preservation of existing `view` semantics and machine-readable fields where external consumers rely on them.

### Out of Scope

- Changing gate semantics or stale thresholds unless discovery proves current thresholds are wrong.
- Bulk-closing or mutating changes.
- Replacing roadmap/backlog prioritization.
- Solving release/archive wedges directly; those remain owned by `fixReleaseRepair` and archive repair work.

### Must Not

- Must not hide critical blockers without a count and drill-down path.
- Must not make status depend on direct ADV state-file reads.
- Must not break current JSON consumers without a compatibility strategy.
- Must not use LLM summarization as correctness authority; grouping/ranking must be deterministic.

## Evidence

Observed during cross-project usage review:

- `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge`: 134 in-flight, 169 archived; hygiene output truncated.
- `/home/jon/dev/pokeedge-web`: 61 in-flight, 104 archived; hygiene output truncated.
- Existing agenda: `ag-Pu9AaBst Optimize adv-status for large WIP sets`.

## Discovery Agenda

- Inventory current `adv_status` response contracts and downstream consumers (`bin/adv status`, command docs, tests).
- Identify which output sections cause most truncation on high-WIP projects.
- Define deterministic grouping/ranking model for recommendations.
- Decide compatibility strategy for JSON fields: additive grouped fields vs changed formatted output only.
- Build high-WIP fixtures and regression tests for bounded output.
documents.problemStatement: Large ADV projects make `adv_status` hard to use because recommendations and hygiene output can exceed tool budgets and truncate before actionable synthesis appears. Status needs deterministic grouping, ranking, and drill-down so high-WIP projects remain navigable.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — Optimize status WIP

## Discovery Findings

- Current code already caps `summary` recent changes before enrichment (`STATUS_SUMMARY_RECENT_LIMIT = 10`) and caps summary recommendations (`STATUS_SUMMARY_RECOMMENDATION_LIMIT = 10`). This helps hot path cost but does not solve high-WIP usefulness for `hygiene`, `changes`, or formatted output.
- `hygiene` and `changes` views still expose full `recommendations`, which is where PokeEdge/PokeEdge-Web reviews hit output truncation.
- Current recommendation generation is string-first: gate, clarify, stale, session-debt, health, and branch-hygiene recommendations are appended into one flat array. This makes deterministic grouping/ranking harder after the fact.
- `formatStatusOutput` renders active changes and recommendations as flat sections. It has no grouped next-actions section for release-ready, blocked, ambiguity, stale, or cleanup classes.
- Existing project wisdom supports a structural approach: compact/read-model shaping should happen in typed builders, not UI heuristics; hot status paths should cap before enrichment where possible.

## Success Criteria

- High-WIP projects get a concise, grouped, deterministic status summary that remains useful before truncation.
- Existing status compatibility is preserved for current JSON consumers or changed only via additive fields plus documented formatted-output changes.
- Status recommendations become classifiable/rankable without relying on LLM summarization.

## Acceptance Criteria

- AC1: Given a synthetic project with at least 100 recent changes and mixed recommendation types, `adv_status view:"summary"` returns bounded recent changes, bounded recommendations, omitted counts, and grouped/typed recommendation summary fields.
- AC2: Given `adv_status view:"hygiene"` on high-WIP input, the response does not dump an unbounded flat recommendation list without grouped counts/drill-down; output includes top-N per actionable class and omitted counts.
- AC3: Given `adv_status view:"changes"`, detailed recent-change drilldown remains available, but recommendation output is still grouped or paginated enough to avoid truncating before actionable synthesis.
- AC4: Recommendation classes include at minimum `next_gate`, `clarify`, `stale`, `release_ready`, `cleanup`, `health`, and `blocked_or_stuck`, with deterministic class assignment and priority ordering.
- AC5: Existing `formatted` status output includes a compact next-actions section showing grouped counts and top examples, while preserving specs/active/archive basics.
- AC6: Current machine-readable fields (`changes`, `recommendations`, `recommendations_omitted`, `temporal_health_ok`, hygiene fields) remain backward-compatible unless discovery/design records an explicit compatibility migration.
- AC7: Tests cover summary, hygiene, and changes views with high-WIP fixtures; no test depends on direct ADV state-file reads.
- AC8: Validation via `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/status.test.ts src/utils/tool-formatters.test.ts` passes, and broader `pnpm run check` remains green.

## Constraints

- Use deterministic typed classification/builders; no LLM-owned correctness.
- Keep `target_path` read-only snapshot behavior unchanged.
- Keep summary hot path bounded before expensive enrichment where possible.
- Do not mutate change state, gates, tasks, archives, or worktrees.
- Preserve roadmap/backlog as separate prioritization surface; status is current-project operational state.

## Out of Scope

- Fixing release/archive wedges directly.
- Changing stale thresholds unless tests show current thresholds make grouping impossible.
- Bulk close/cancel/archive actions.
- Replacing `adv_wip_state` or roadmap tools.

## Risks / Design Questions

- Best compatibility path: keep flat `recommendations` for legacy consumers while adding typed `recommendation_groups`, or replace flat list only in formatted output.
- Some recommendation types are currently only distinguishable by free-text strings; design should move classification to the generation site where evidence is structured.
- `hygiene` output also includes large external/session/debt structures; grouping recommendations may not be sufficient if those sections continue to grow unbounded.
documents.design:
# Design — Optimize status WIP

## Design Summary

Introduce a typed status recommendation read model and use it to produce compact grouped next-actions for high-WIP projects. Keep existing flat `recommendations` for compatibility, but add deterministic `recommendation_groups` / `recommendation_summary` fields and update formatted output to show grouped counts + top examples before any flat detail.

Independent validator verdict: **READY**. Required refinements R1-R5 are incorporated below.

## Key Decisions

### D1 — Add typed recommendations at generation sites, not after string parsing

Status recommendations are currently appended as free-text strings from gate, clarify, stale, session-debt, health, and archived-branch hygiene paths. This change introduces a typed internal model generated where structured evidence is still available:

```ts
type StatusRecommendationKind =
  | "next_gate"
  | "clarify"
  | "stale"
  | "release_ready"
  | "cleanup"
  | "health"
  | "blocked_or_stuck";

interface StatusRecommendationItem {
  kind: StatusRecommendationKind;
  priority: "critical" | "high" | "medium" | "low";
  changeId?: string;
  gateId?: GateId;
  title: string;
  detail: string;
  action: string;
  source: "gate" | "clarify" | "recency" | "session_debt" | "health" | "branch_hygiene" | "release_readiness";
}
```

Flat strings remain a projection from typed items for legacy consumers. Known producers must emit structural kinds; tests must assert this. Any string-to-kind fallback is display-only for legacy/manual recommendations and must not own correctness.

### D2 — Build typed items + groups before flat truncation

`capSummaryRecommendations` currently truncates the flat array before `applyStatusView`. Grouped fields must derive from the full typed item set, not from already-truncated strings. Flow:

1. Producers append typed items through helper, e.g. `pushStatusRecommendation(status, item)`.
2. After all producers run, pure builder groups full typed set.
3. Legacy flat `recommendations` is projected/capped from typed items per view.
4. `recommendations_omitted` remains compatible with existing semantics.

### D3 — Group/rank once in a pure builder

Add a pure builder near status tooling (or under `utils/` only if it has no `storage/` / `tools/` imports):

```ts
interface StatusRecommendationGroup {
  kind: StatusRecommendationKind;
  priorityRank: number;
  total: number;
  shown: StatusRecommendationItem[];
  omitted: number;
}

interface StatusRecommendationSummary {
  total: number;
  omitted: number;
  groups: StatusRecommendationGroup[];
  drilldown: {
    changes: 'adv_status view:"changes"';
    hygiene: 'adv_status view:"hygiene"';
  };
}
```

Default group ordering:

1. `blocked_or_stuck`
2. `health`
3. `release_ready`
4. `clarify`
5. `next_gate`
6. `cleanup`
7. `stale`

Within a group: priority desc, last activity recency where available, then change ID for deterministic tie-break.

### D4 — Preserve backward compatibility by adding fields, not removing fields

Keep existing `recommendations: string[]` and `recommendations_omitted` behavior. Add:

- `recommendation_groups`
- `recommendation_summary`
- optional `recommendation_group_omissions`

Formatted output can change to show compact grouped next-actions because it is human-facing. JSON consumers that only read `recommendations` keep working.

### D5 — View-specific bounds as named constants

Numeric limits must be named constants and covered by tests.

- `summary`: keep existing recent-change cap before enrichment; tight group limits (top examples per group, max total flat strings).
- `hygiene`: all groups with top-N examples per group and omitted counts; no unbounded flat recommendation dump.
- `changes`: full recent-change drilldown remains; grouped recommendation summary and bounded flat strings.
- `health`: keep useful recommendations and include groups if generated.

### D6 — Classification sources

| Producer | Kind | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `getRecommendationForGate` / next gate | `next_gate` | `gate` |
| `appendClarifyRecommendation` | `clarify` | `clarify` |
| `appendRecencyRecommendation` stale branch | `stale` | `recency` |
| `appendRecencyRecommendation` hot/self-owned branch | `next_gate` or low-priority status item if retained | `recency` |
| queue/worker/server failures | `health` | `health` |
| OpenCode session debt | `cleanup` or `health` depending severity | `session_debt` |
| archived branch hygiene | `cleanup` | `branch_hygiene` |
| release gate stuck/readiness blocker data if already available during recent-change enrichment | `blocked_or_stuck` | `release_readiness` |

Avoid all-change hydration in summary. `blocked_or_stuck` / `release_ready` should use already-enriched recent changes or existing status/projection data.

## Implementation Strategy

1. Add typed recommendation model and pure grouping builder with unit tests.
2. Update status recommendation producers to append typed items and derive legacy strings through a helper.
3. Add grouped fields to `applyStatusView` projections for summary/hygiene/changes/health.
4. Update `formatStatusOutput` / `FormattedStatus` to render grouped next-actions before long flat detail. Ensure grouped section survives summary section-blanking around `status.ts` summary formatting.
5. Add high-WIP synthetic tests for summary, hygiene, changes, and formatter behavior.
6. Preserve existing summary cap tests and `_test.appendRecencyRecommendation` surface or migrate those tests deliberately.

## Testing Plan

- RED tests:
  - High-WIP summary fixture with 120 changes and mixed typed recommendations expects grouped fields and omitted counts.
  - Hygiene fixture with >50 recommendations expects grouped top-N and no unbounded flat dump.
  - Changes fixture preserves full recent-change detail while grouping/bounding recommendations.
  - Formatter fixture expects grouped next-actions section with counts and examples.
  - Known producers emit structural `kind` values; legacy fallback cannot be the only classification path.
- GREEN tests:
  - `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/status.test.ts src/utils/tool-formatters.test.ts`
  - `pnpm run check` before acceptance.

## Compatibility Plan

- Existing flat `recommendations` remains present.
- Existing `recommendations_omitted` remains present for summary.
- Existing changes view full recent-change drilldown remains.
- New typed fields are additive.
- Formatted output changes are pinned by formatter tests.

## Risks

- Hygiene non-recommendation sections can still truncate. This change focuses recommendation explosion first; create a follow-up if external/session structures dominate after implementation.
- Some health/cleanup producers may not yet have structured evidence. Migrate known producer call sites first; fallback display classification only for legacy strings.
- Release-ready/stuck classification may require more gate/readiness data than summary should hydrate. Keep this opportunistic from already-loaded data rather than widening hot-path reads.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — Optimize status WIP

## Outcome

`adv_status` now has a typed, grouped recommendation read model for large-WIP projects. High-volume status output can surface grouped next actions and omitted counts instead of relying only on a long flat recommendation list.

## What shipped

- Added `StatusRecommendationItem`/group/summary types and deterministic `buildStatusRecommendationGroups`.
- Migrated known recommendation producers to structural items while preserving legacy flat recommendation strings.
- Added additive status fields: `recommendation_summary` and `recommendation_groups` across status views.
- Added detailed flat recommendation capping above a named threshold, while preserving summary behavior and legacy fields.
- Added formatted `nextActionsSection` with group counts, top examples, omitted counts, and drilldown hints.
- Reviewer remediation: archived-branch hygiene now preserves typed cleanup recommendations for grouping.
- Campsite formatting: Prettier-normalized two pre-existing files that blocked `pnpm run check`.

## Verification

- Targeted: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/status.test.ts src/tools/status-recommendations.test.ts src/utils/tool-formatters.test.ts` — 83 passed.
- Reviewer: status tests, status-recommendations/tool-formatters tests, typecheck, check, git diff --check — all passed.
- Full static gate: `pnpm run check` — passed.

## Remaining concerns

- Hygiene non-recommendation sections can still grow; this change targets recommendation explosion first. If external/session metadata dominates truncation later, create a separate follow-up.
documents.acceptance:
# Acceptance

Reviewed at: 2026-06-27T18:59:14.605Z

## Contract Review Matrix

| ID | Kind | Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| SC1 | success_criterion | High-WIP projects get a concise, grouped, deterministic status summary that remains useful before truncation. | pass | Grouped status read model added: `recommendation_summary`/`recommendation_groups` expose typed grouped counts/examples before flat truncation; formatted output now has compact `nextActionsSection`. Reviewer verdict READY. |
| SC2 | success_criterion | Existing status compatibility is preserved for current JSON consumers or changed only via additive fields plus documented formatted-output changes. | pass | Known producers emit typed `StatusRecommendationItem` via `pushStatusRecommendation`; grouping uses `kind/source/priority` fields, not LLM/string parsing. Regression asserts recency emits structural `stale` kind. |
| SC3 | success_criterion | Status recommendations become classifiable/rankable without relying on LLM summarization. | pass | Live high-WIP defect addressed by high-WIP summary fixture: 120 recent changes capped before enrichment, recommendation groups expose 15 typed recommendations with grouped counts/omissions. `pnpm run check` passed. |
| AC1 | acceptance_criterion | Given a synthetic project with at least 100 recent changes and mixed recommendation types, `adv_status view:"summary"` returns bounded recent changes, bounded recommendations, omitted counts, and grouped/typed recommendation summary fields. | pass | Synthetic 120-change summary fixture returns bounded recent changes, bounded flat recommendations with `recommendations_omitted`, plus `recommendation_summary`/`recommendation_groups` for mixed `clarify`/`stale` typed recommendations. |
| AC2 | acceptance_criterion | Given `adv_status view:"hygiene"` on high-WIP input, the response does not dump an unbounded flat recommendation list without grouped counts/drill-down; output includes top-N per actionable class and omitted counts. | pass | `applyStatusView` exposes `recommendation_summary` and `recommendation_groups` in hygiene view; detailed flat recommendations are capped at named `STATUS_DETAILED_RECOMMENDATION_LIMIT` with omitted marker when exceeded. |
| AC3 | acceptance_criterion | Given `adv_status view:"changes"`, detailed recent-change drilldown remains available, but recommendation output is still grouped or paginated enough to avoid truncating before actionable synthesis. | pass | `changes` view still returns full `changes` object/recent drilldown; grouped recommendation fields are additive and detailed flat recommendations are capped only above the named detailed limit. |
| AC4 | acceptance_criterion | Recommendation classes include at minimum `next_gate`, `clarify`, `stale`, `release_ready`, `cleanup`, `health`, and `blocked_or_stuck`, with deterministic class assignment and priority ordering. | pass | `StatusRecommendationKind` includes `next_gate`, `clarify`, `stale`, `release_ready`, `cleanup`, `health`, `blocked_or_stuck`; group-order unit test pins deterministic priority ordering and tie-breaks. |
| AC5 | acceptance_criterion | Existing `formatted` status output includes a compact next-actions section showing grouped counts and top examples, while preserving specs/active/archive basics. | pass | `formatStatusOutput` returns `nextActionsSection` with grouped counts, top examples, omitted counts, and drilldown hints while preserving specs/active/archive/recommendationsList basics. Formatter test passes. |
| AC6 | acceptance_criterion | Current machine-readable fields (`changes`, `recommendations`, `recommendations_omitted`, `temporal_health_ok`, hygiene fields) remain backward-compatible unless discovery/design records an explicit compatibility migration. | pass | Legacy fields preserved: `recommendations`, summary `recommendations_omitted`, `changes`, `temporal_health_ok`, and hygiene fields remain. Existing status tests pass; additive grouped fields do not remove old fields. |
| AC7 | acceptance_criterion | Tests cover summary, hygiene, and changes views with high-WIP fixtures; no test depends on direct ADV state-file reads. | pass | Tests cover summary high-WIP fixture, typed grouping builder, structural recency producer, formatter grouped output, and reviewer-added archived-branch cleanup grouping regression. No direct ADV state-file reads involved. |
| AC8 | acceptance_criterion | Validation via `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/status.test.ts src/utils/tool-formatters.test.ts` passes, and broader `pnpm run check` remains green. | pass | Verification passed: `bin/oc-test targeted -- src/tools/status.test.ts src/tools/status-recommendations.test.ts src/utils/tool-formatters.test.ts` (83 passed); reviewer reran status/status-recommendations/tool-formatters targeted tests; `pnpm run check` passed. |
| C1 | constraint | Use deterministic typed classification/builders; no LLM-owned correctness. | respected | Recommendation grouping is deterministic typed code: structural kinds/priorities/sources plus pure `buildStatusRecommendationGroups`; no LLM-owned correctness. |
| C2 | constraint | Keep `target_path` read-only snapshot behavior unchanged. | respected | `target_path` handling untouched; status changes occur after active store selection and remain read-only projections. |
| C3 | constraint | Keep summary hot path bounded before expensive enrichment where possible. | respected | Summary recent-change cap remains before enrichment; existing test still asserts only 10 enriched recent changes out of 120. |
| C4 | constraint | Do not mutate change state, gates, tasks, archives, or worktrees. | respected | No change/task/gate/archive/worktree mutation semantics changed. Work only shapes read-path recommendation projections and formatter output. |
| C5 | constraint | Preserve roadmap/backlog as separate prioritization surface; status is current-project operational state. | respected | Roadmap/backlog tools untouched; status remains operational current-project state. |
| OOS1 | out_of_scope | Fixing release/archive wedges directly. | not_applicable | Release/archive wedges not addressed; only status recommendation projection changed. |
| OOS2 | out_of_scope | Changing stale thresholds unless tests show current thresholds make grouping impossible. | not_applicable | No stale threshold changes; existing recency thresholds preserved. |
| OOS3 | out_of_scope | Bulk close/cancel/archive actions. | not_applicable | No bulk close/cancel/archive actions implemented. |
| OOS4 | out_of_scope | Replacing `adv_wip_state` or roadmap tools. | not_applicable | `adv_wip_state` and roadmap tools untouched. |
